messages.author: RedTop
messages.text: RedTop: Hello ... my first post on Discord. I have a Heltec V4 and found on github an alternative firmware to flash my V4 with. It's supposed to help reduce current consumption. The github site also mentions that a simple board modification will improve receiver sensitivity . I'm reluctant to do that until I get more info on both the alt. firmware and the board mod. Anyone here that can give me some advice on this subject? Thanks. Here's the link to that github site: p.s. pardon me if this has already been addressed. https://github.com/dt267/MeshCore-Low-Power-Firmware-For-Heltec-V3-V4
messages.content_html: RedTop: Hello ... my first post on Discord. I have a Heltec V4 and found on github an alternative firmware to flash my V4 with. It's supposed to help reduce current consumption. The github site also mentions that a simple board modification will improve receiver sensitivity . I'm reluctant to do that until I get more info on both the alt. firmware and the board mod. Anyone here that can give me some advice on this subject? Thanks. Here's the link to that github site: p.s. pardon me if this has already been addressed. https://github.com/dt267/MeshCore-Low-Power-Firmware-For-Heltec-V3-V4
embeds_html:
GitHub - dt267/MeshCore-Low-Power-Firmware-For-Heltec-V3-V4: Optimi...
Optimized MeshCore firmware, engineered for low power consumption and extended off-grid battery life for multi-day operation. - dt267/MeshCore-Low-Power-Firmware-For-Heltec-V3-V4

messages.author: Fokai
messages.text: We've been using this in the PNW with success. However, the bug with 1.14 that was discovered and now in dev has not yet made it's way into PS14 I've linked. https://github.com/IoTThinks/EasySkyMesh/releases As far as your hardware mod, it works well if your base noise floor is worse than ~95-97dB. (edited)
messages.content_html: We've been using this in the PNW with success. However, the bug with 1.14 that was discovered and now in dev has not yet made it's way into PS14 I've linked. https://github.com/IoTThinks/EasySkyMesh/releases As far as your hardware mod, it works well if your base noise floor is worse than ~95-97dB. (edited)
embeds_html:
Releases · IoTThinks/EasySkyMesh
A sharing place to form IoT sensor networks via LoRa Mesh technology. - IoTThinks/EasySkyMesh

messages.author: RedTop
messages.text: Hi Fokai. I'm quite new to this and have questions on what you wrote. Are you suggesting I could do better by flashing the code at the link you've provided? I don't understand about the bug and its relation to my present flash or the one you link to. Also, my present NF is -102, so does that mean I should leave the board alone or do the mod? Thanks for your help and reply.
messages.content_html: Hi Fokai. I'm quite new to this and have questions on what you wrote. Are you suggesting I could do better by flashing the code at the link you've provided? I don't understand about the bug and its relation to my present flash or the one you link to. Also, my present NF is -102, so does that mean I should leave the board alone or do the mod? Thanks for your help and reply.
messages.reply_html:
Fokai
We've been using this in the PNW with success. However, the bug with 1.14 that was discovered and now in dev has not yet made it's way into PS14 I've linked. https://github.com/IoTThinks/EasySkyMesh/releases As far as your hardware mod, it works well if your base noise floor is worse than ~95-97dB. (edited)

messages.author: Fokai
messages.text: If you are at -102 I would leave the LNA and not bypass. It should work fine at that level. Sorry I didn't ask, but you are looking to run this on a companion or repeater? PS14 isn't released for companion yet on the linked site. 1.14 bug has to do with 2-byte and adverts, which I saw chatter the fix might be updated in a new firmware (main) by the end of the week. I wouldn't worry about it for now as it hopefully will be fixed soon.
messages.content_html: If you are at -102 I would leave the LNA and not bypass. It should work fine at that level. Sorry I didn't ask, but you are looking to run this on a companion or repeater? PS14 isn't released for companion yet on the linked site. 1.14 bug has to do with 2-byte and adverts, which I saw chatter the fix might be updated in a new firmware (main) by the end of the week. I wouldn't worry about it for now as it hopefully will be fixed soon.

messages.author: RedTop
messages.text: Ok on just leaving the board alone, I'll take your advice. You brought up a good question. I noticed on your link that the Companion isn't ready yet. I'm not sure if this board is going to stay a companion or if I'm going to change it to a rptr.
messages.content_html: Ok on just leaving the board alone, I'll take your advice. You brought up a good question. I noticed on your link that the Companion isn't ready yet. I'm not sure if this board is going to stay a companion or if I'm going to change it to a rptr.

messages.author: Alfons
messages.text: Hi, I have a Heltec v4.3 . The noise floor doesn't go below -92dB, either with Heltec's compiled FW, or with specific v4.3 compiles I found elsewhere. Any ideas?
messages.content_html: Hi, I have a Heltec v4.3 . The noise floor doesn't go below -92dB, either with Heltec's compiled FW, or with specific v4.3 compiles I found elsewhere. Any ideas?
